items.title: New leaders, new fund: Sequoia has raised $7B to expand its AI bets
items.summary: The fundraise is the first major capital raise under Sequoia's new leadership, with Alfred Lin and Pat Grady now serving as co-stewards of the 54-year-old firm.

items.title: Factory hits $1.5B valuation to build AI coding for enterprises
items.summary: The three-year-old startup raised $150 million led by Khosla Ventures.

items.title: Luma launches AI-powered production studio with faith-focused Wonder Project
items.summary: The studio's first project will be about Moses and star Academy Award-winner Ben Kingsley, to be released this spring on Prime Video.

items.title: Netflix co-founder and chair Reed Hastings to leave board
items.summary: Hastings helped to transform the video rental industry — first with physical, then digital, delivery.

items.title: Upscale AI in talks to raise at $2B valuation, says report
items.summary: AI infrastructure company Upscale AI is reportedly in talks to raise its third funding round since launching just seven months ago.

items.title: Physical Intelligence, a hot robotics startup, says its new robot brain can figure out tasks it was never taught
items.summary: The new model, called π0.7, represents what the company describes as an early but meaningful step toward the long-sought goal of a general-purpose robot brain.

items.title: From the Startup Battlefield stage to the International Space Station: geCKo Materials built a sticky product
items.summary: This week on Build Mode, Isabelle Johannessen speaks with Capella Kerst, founder and CEO of geCKo Materials.

items.title: Slash, a Ramp competitor founded by teenagers, raises $100M at $1.4B valuation
items.summary: Five years later the founders, now 24 years old, say their startup has achieved $300 million in annualized revenue.

items.title: OpenAI takes aim at Anthropic with beefed-up Codex that gives it more power over your desktop
items.summary: OpenAI's agentic coding tool has gotten a major makeover, with a variety of new powers and abilities.

items.title: European police email 75,000 people asking them to stop DDoS attacks
items.summary: Europol coordinated an operation against for-hire distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) services, including the arrest of four people and the takedown of 53 domains.

items.title: Anthropic CPO leaves Figma’s board after reports he will offer a competing product
items.summary: Krieger's departure and any forthcoming design tools will be another data point for investors who fear the SaaSpocalypse — that the largest AI labs will come to dominate software businesses, a thesis that has rocked public markets at times this year.

items.title: It’s not just you — Bluesky is (sorta) down
items.summary: Bluesky has been experiencing ongoing service disruptions since just before 3 a.m. ET.

items.title: Everything we like is a psyop
items.summary: If culture is shaped by TikTok's algorithmic feed, then it's time for us to decide where we draw the line between necessary marketing and inauthentic growth hacking.

items.title: Google now lets you explore the web side-by-side with AI Mode
items.summary: Now, when you're using AI Mode on Chrome desktop, clicking a link will open the web page side-by-side with AI Mode.

items.title: Two Americans sentenced for helping North Korea steal $5 million in fake IT worker scheme
items.summary: The U.S. Department of Justice announced that two Americans were sentenced to years in prison for helping the North Korean government place fake IT workers in U.S. companies.

items.title: InsightFinder raises $15M to help companies figure out where AI agents go wrong
items.summary: According to CEO Helen Gu, the biggest problem facing the industry today is not just monitoring and diagnosing where AI models go wrong — it's also diagnosing how the entire tech stack operates now that AI is part of it.

items.title: AI traffic to US retailers rose 393% in Q1, and it’s boosting their revenue too
items.summary: Adobe says AI traffic to U.S. retail sites also jumped 269% in March, with visitors converting better and generating more revenue than non-AI shoppers.

items.title: Roblox’s AI assistant gets new agentic tools to plan, build, and test games
items.summary: The new tools are designed to help creators throughout the entire development process.

items.title: Google adds Nano Banana-powered image generation to Gemini’s Personal Intelligence
items.summary: That means its AI images can be created using Gemini's understanding of your likes and interests, without those having to be explicitly noted in the prompt.

items.title: Google is now targeting bad ads over bad actors
items.summary: Google blocked 8.3 billion ads in 2025 but suspended fewer advertisers.
